notes: Explicit transferFeeConfig case in the validator and token-extension renderer.
transferHook / transferHookAccount cases handled and rendered with description and links.
defaultAccountState case present in the renderer.
permanentDelegate case present in the renderer.
nonTransferable / nonTransferableAccount cases present.
interestBearingConfig case present.
cpiGuard case present.
memoTransfer case present.
confidentialTransferMint / confidentialTransferAccount cases exist and are labeled; encrypted balances/amounts are ElGamal ciphertext and cannot be shown as plaintext.
confidentialTransferFeeConfig / confidentialTransferFeeAmount cases present; withheld confidential fee amounts remain encrypted.
metadataPointer case present.
tokenMetadata case present; name/symbol/URI rendered.
groupPointer case present.
tokenGroup case present in the switch.
groupMemberPointer case present.
tokenGroupMember case present.
mintCloseAuthority case present.
scaledUiAmountConfig case added (PR #598); a dedicated multiplier tooltip renders the value.
pausableConfig / pausableAccount cases added (PR #598) and rendered with description and links.
A default/unparseableExtension case returns 'Unknown Extension' so unrecognized extensions are still listed gracefully.

Solana Explorer is open-source; verdicts are derived directly from source (app/validators/accounts/token-extension.ts enumerates all extension types; app/utils/token-extension.ts renders each, plus an unparseableExtension fallback).
It is fully read-only; nothing is blocked or executed. All 20 extension kinds have an explicit case, including scaledUiAmountConfig and pausableConfig (PR #598).
Confidential transfer extensions are partial only because encrypted ElGamal balances/amounts are inherently undecodable; the extension presence and config fields are still shown.
